title: penstemon heterodoxus var. cephalophorus
intro: Penstemon heterodoxus var. cephalophorus, also known as the Southern Beardtongue, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. This plant is native to the southeastern United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of the Southern Beardtongue include its ability to reduce fever, treat respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. Southern Beardtongue can be prepared for use in herbal remedies through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments.
